acceptance_criteria: Frontmatter schema is declared in a single source-of-truth file checked into the repo.
Validator loads each kept article's YAML frontmatter and reports per-article violations against the schema.
Validator exits non-zero on any violation and is wired into CI.
Validator consumes the sibling-repo path through the AB#3132 storage abstraction (no hard-coded paths).
New unit tests cover: valid article passes; missing required field fails; wrong-typed field fails; unknown extra field is handled per the chosen strictness policy (documented in the schema file).
Build passes with zero errors and warnings; all existing tests pass.
description:
Declare the Obsidian-compatible YAML frontmatter schema for kept articles in the sibling research repo as a single source-of-truth file (location and format to be chosen by the implementer; the schema must be both human-readable and machine-loadable). Implement a validator that walks every kept article via the AB#3132 storage abstraction, parses its frontmatter, and checks the shape against the schema. Validator exits non-zero with a per-article report on violation. Wire the validator into CI so frontmatter drift fails the build.

The schema must accommodate the frontmatter fields AB#3135 already emits; if AB#3135's emitted shape disagrees with what this Task wants to declare, file a follow-up bug rather than silently widening the schema to accept both.
title: Frontmatter schema declaration + validator for kept articles

acceptance_criteria: Resolver parses `[[target]]`, `[[target|alias]]`, and `[[target#heading]]` forms.
Resolver verifies each target maps to an existing kept article under the Johnny-Decimal layout used by AB#3135.
Dangling links produce a per-link report (article path + link text) and a non-zero exit code.
Resolver consumes the sibling-repo path through the AB#3132 storage abstraction.
Resolver is wired into CI.
New unit tests cover: plain link resolves; aliased link resolves; heading link resolves; dangling link fails; case/spacing edge cases match the chosen Obsidian-compatible normalisation rule.
Build passes with zero errors and warnings; all existing tests pass.
description: Implement a resolver that parses every `[[target]]` reference in the body of every kept article and verifies the target resolves to an existing article in the sibling research repo. Must handle the Obsidian variants `[[target]]`, `[[target|alias]]`, and `[[target#heading]]`. Honour the Johnny-Decimal numbering layout adopted by AB#3135 so that linking by number-prefixed slug works as expected and renames-by-renumber are detected as breakages. Exits non-zero with a per-link report on dangling targets. Wire into CI so a broken `[[link]]` fails the build.
title: Wiki-link resolver for kept articles (Obsidian-compatible)

acceptance_criteria: Canonical citation representation is declared (location and shape) and documented alongside the frontmatter schema from task-1.
Validator detects every citation in a kept article and asserts URL and ISO-8601 capture date are present.
Missing-field violations produce a per-citation report (article path + offending citation) and a non-zero exit code.
Validator consumes the sibling-repo path through the AB#3132 storage abstraction.
Validator is wired into CI.
New unit tests cover: valid citation passes; missing URL fails; missing/malformed capture date fails; date in non-ISO-8601 form fails.
Build passes with zero errors and warnings; all existing tests pass.
description:
Implement a validator that walks every kept article and asserts that each citation includes at minimum a source URL and a capture date in ISO-8601 form. Citation shape (frontmatter field, dedicated section, or inline pattern) is determined by what AB#3135 actually emits; this Task picks the canonical representation, declares it, and validates against it. Exits non-zero with a per-citation report on missing fields. Wire into CI.

If AB#3135's emitted citation shape lacks one of these required fields, file a follow-up bug rather than relaxing the rule here.
title: Citation rigor validator (URL + capture-date minimum)

acceptance_criteria: `INDEX.md` regenerator walks kept articles deterministically (sorted by Johnny-Decimal number then slug; no reliance on filesystem enumeration order).
Output contains no run-time-varying content (no timestamps, no machine identifiers, no locale-sensitive formatting).
Re-running the regenerator on the same input set produces a byte-identical `INDEX.md`.
CI check asserts byte-identical regeneration (running the regenerator must leave the working tree clean).
Regenerator consumes the sibling-repo path through the AB#3132 storage abstraction.
If AB#3135 already provides an `INDEX.md` writer, this Task refactors it in place rather than introducing a parallel one.
New unit tests cover: deterministic ordering across multiple inputs; byte-identical re-run; stable behaviour when articles are added/removed/renumbered.
Build passes with zero errors and warnings; all existing tests pass.
description:
Replace any nondeterministic behaviour in the kept-article `INDEX.md` writer (filesystem-order traversal, locale-sensitive sort, timestamped headers, etc.) with a fully-ordered, stable serialisation: deterministic walk over kept articles via the AB#3132 storage abstraction, deterministic sort key (Johnny-Decimal number then slug), stable line endings, and no embedded run-time metadata. Provide a single entry-point regenerator command/function. Add a CI check that runs the regenerator twice and asserts the output is byte-identical, and that re-running it over an unchanged input set leaves `INDEX.md` byte-identical to the committed file.

If AB#3135 already ships an `INDEX.md` writer, refactor it in place rather than introducing a parallel one.
title: Deterministic `INDEX.md` regenerator + byte-stability check
